role_title: Strategic Partnerships & Growth Lead — COCOM / SOF

level_signals: 5+ years of channel sales, BD, or partnership management experience
Described as 'a senior individual contributor role with revenue ownership'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Active TS/SCI — candidates without an active TS/SCI will not be considered
5+ years of channel sales, BD, or partnership management experience inside the defense industrial base with direct exposure to COCOM or SOF-aligned programs
Existing relationships at one or more prime contractors or technology companies active in the COCOM and SOF market at the BD, program management, or technical leadership level
Understanding of SOCOM's unique acquisition environment: MFP-11 budget authority, SOF AT&L, SOFWERX rapid prototyping, and OTA mechanisms specific to USSOCOM and the geographic COCOMs
Track record of standing up or expanding channel and partner relationships that drove material software or technology revenue ($500K+) in the COCOM or SOF ecosystem
Comfortable navigating both the prime-facing channel layer (BD, program managers, contracting) and the end-user pull-through layer (SOF developers, J6 tech leads, command innovation offices)

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Prior active duty or civilian service in a SOF unit, JSOC, SOCOM, or geographic COCOM in a software, data, or technology capacity
Experience selling developer tools, AI/ML platforms, or SDLC tooling through or alongside SOF-focused primes or technology companies
Existing relationships at USSOCOM (MacDill), JSOC (Fort Liberty), AFSOC (Hurlburt), or a geographic COCOM J6 or innovation office
Familiarity with SOCOM's technical community: SOFWERX, NavalX portfolio, SOF innovation challenges, and rapid prototyping mechanisms
Network within the SOF prime ecosystem: Booz Allen BSIM, Leidos National Security Sector, CACI Intelligence & Technology Solutions, L3Harris Communication Systems

explicitly_excluded: Candidates without an active TS/SCI will not be considered

scope.surface: COCOM and SOF channel — prime contractor partnerships, end-user pull-through, and revenue ownership for Cursor's defense community motion
scope.business_model: B2B / channel sales into defense industrial base and government
core_responsibilities: Own and grow Cursor's relationships with prime contractors and integrators focused on COCOM and SOF programs
Negotiate and close partnership agreements, reseller arrangements, teaming agreements, and approved vendor designations
Work with prime BD and program management teams to co-sell Cursor into new COCOM and SOF contract awards at the proposal stage
Build relationships inside USSOCOM, JSOC, AFSOC, MARSOC, NSWC, and the geographic COCOMs
Engage SOF AT&L, USSOCOM's acquisition community, and command innovation offices
Represent Cursor at SOF Week, SOFIC, AUSA Global Force Symposium, and AFCEA events
Own a revenue target for COCOM and SOF channel business
Build and manage a pipeline of prime contractor partnerships and program-level co-sell opportunities
Forecast and report on channel pipeline
Partner with Field Engineering on technical evaluations and POCs inside prime contractor and SOF developer environments
Channel SOF developer feedback and mission-critical product requirements back to Cursor's roadmap team

green_flags: Highly specific role with clear ownership — defined channel, defined partners, defined acquisition mechanisms
Senior IC role reporting directly to sales leadership — suggests real authority and access
SOF/COCOM procurement environment described as moving faster than conventional DoD acquisition — urgency signal
Cursor already has organic usage inside these communities — not a cold start
Compensation not stated but Cursor is a high-profile AI company likely offering competitive packages
red_flags: Active TS/SCI is a hard gate — eliminates the vast majority of candidates
No location or remote policy stated — unclear if role requires presence near MacDill, Fort Liberty, or elsewhere
Very narrow addressable candidate pool given the combination of TS/SCI + SOF/COCOM BD experience + tech sales background
Role sits at intersection of BD, government relations, and channel sales — archetype is non-standard and hard to classify

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (not stated; left as 'unclear' — no city or remote policy specified)
location.work_auth (inferred as us-citizenship-required due to mandatory active TS/SCI clearance)
location.visa_sponsorship (inferred as 'no' given TS/SCI hard requirement)

ambiguities: Archetype does not cleanly fit any defined enum — this is a defense-focused channel BD/partnerships role, not a traditional BizOps, Growth, or PM role. 'BizOps / Strategy & Ops' selected as closest approximation; archetype_confidence set to low.

Location is entirely unstated — the role references MacDill AFB, Fort Liberty, and Hurlburt Field as customer sites but does not specify where the employee would be based or whether remote is permitted.
Work authorization: TS/SCI strongly implies US citizenship is required, but the JD does not explicitly state citizenship as a requirement — only active clearance.
